heading: Institution of post-grant review
label: 324 Institution of post-grant review
notes: Effective DateSection effective upon the expiration of the 1-year period beginning , and applicable only to patents described in ( note), with certain exceptions and limitations, see section 6(f)(2), (3) of , set out as a note under .Sept. 16, 2011section 3(n)(1) of Pub. L. 112–2935 U.S.C. 100Pub. L. 112–29section 321 of this title

children.heading: .—Threshold
children.label: a .—Threshold
children.text: The Director may not authorize a post-grant review to be instituted unless the Director determines that the information presented in the petition filed under section 321, if such information is not rebutted, would demonstrate that it is more likely than not that at least 1 of the claims challenged in the petition is unpatentable.

children.heading: .—Additional Grounds
children.label: b .—Additional Grounds
children.text: The determination required under subsection (a) may also be satisfied by a showing that the petition raises a novel or unsettled legal question that is important to other patents or patent applications.
receiving a preliminary response to the petition under section 323; or
if no such preliminary response is filed, the last date on which such response may be filed.

children.heading: .—Notice
children.label: d .—Notice
children.text: The Director shall notify the petitioner and patent owner, in writing, of the Director’s determination under subsection (a) or (b), and shall make such notice available to the public as soon as is practicable. Such notice shall include the date on which the review shall commence.

children.heading: .—No Appeal
children.label: e .—No Appeal
children.text: The determination by the Director whether to institute a post-grant review under this section shall be final and nonappealable.
